390:| AC-FEAT-003-15 | Rapid-MLX utilization is recorded from a real macOS Apple Silicon Rapid-MLX server with `go-vcr`: record mode requires `FIZEAU_RECORD_PROVIDER_CASSETTES=1` and `RAPID_MLX_RECORD_BASE_URL`, records `/v1/models`, `/v1/status`, minimal chat, and under-load status evidence when feasible; replay mode is default and parses running, waiting, total prompt/completion counters, Metal memory, cache details, and active-request timing fields from the cassette. | `go test ./internal/provider/rapidmlx ./internal/provider/... -run 'Rapid.*Utilization|Rapid.*Cassette|Utilization'`; `FIZEAU_RECORD_PROVIDER_CASSETTES=1 RAPID_MLX_RECORD_BASE_URL=http://host:8000/v1 go test ./internal/provider/rapidmlx -run 'Record'` |

verification_command passed: go test ./internal/provider/rapidmlx ./internal/provider/... -run 'Rapid.*Utilization|Rapid.*Cassette|Utilization' && rg -n 'AC-FEAT-003-15' docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-003-providers.md
rationale: verification_command: go test ./internal/provider/rapidmlx ./internal/provider/... -run 'Rapid.*Utilization|Rapid.*Cassette|Utilization' && rg -n 'AC-FEAT-003-15' docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-003-providers.md

The Rapid-MLX utilization bead is already implemented in the current worktree, so no code changes were necessary.

Evidence:
- `internal/provider/rapidmlx/utilization_probe.go` parses `/v1/status`, derives `http://host:8000/v1/status` via `utilization.ServerRoot`, normalizes active/queued requests, prompt/completion totals, cache fields, Metal memory, and active-request timing fields, and falls back to stale/unknown utilization on probe failure.
- `internal/provider/rapidmlx/utilization_probe_test.go` covers normalized parsing, stale-or-unknown failure behavior, replay cassette coverage, and record-mode gating via `FIZEAU_RECORD_PROVIDER_CASSETTES` plus `RAPID_MLX_RECORD_BASE_URL`.
- `internal/provider/rapidmlx/testdata/cassettes/rapidmlx_utilization.yaml` includes `/v1/models`, `/status`, minimal chat, and under-load `/status` evidence.
- `docs/helix/01-frame/features/FEAT-003-providers.md` includes AC-FEAT-003-15 for Rapid-MLX utilization.
